channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Benue gov promises Tinubu 620,000 votes
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Benue State Governor, Hyacinth Alia, promises President Bola Tinubu 620,000 votes in the 2027 election, doubling 2023 figures. Read more on Benue votes.
Read More: https://punchng.com/benue-gov-promises-tinubu-620000-votes/

item.title: Police impound 81 vehicles over covered, defaced number plates in Kano
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Kano State Police Command impounds 81 vehicles for covered or defaced number plates, warning of prosecution. This operation aims to curb security threats i
Read More: https://punchng.com/police-impound-81-vehicles-over-covered-defaced-number-plates-in-kano/

item.title: 32 killed, 26 injured in three road crashes across Kogi, Ogun, Kwara — FRSC
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Tragic road crashes in Kogi, Ogun, and Kwara states claim 32 lives and injure 26. FRSC blames speeding, fatigue, and dangerous driving for the preventable
Read More: https://punchng.com/32-killed-26-injured-in-three-road-crashes-across-kogi-ogun-kwara-frsc/

item.title: INEC, ICPC train staff in corruption-free 2027 elections
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
INEC and ICPC partner to train staff on integrity and anti-corruption measures, ensuring free and fair 2027 elections in Nigeria. Learn more about the init
Read More: https://punchng.com/inec-icpc-train-staff-in-corruption-free-2027-elections/

item.title: Guinea-Bissau junta to hold constitutional referendum August 30
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Guinea-Bissau’s military junta will hold a constitutional referendum on August 30, 2026, to approve changes giving the president more powers.
Read More: https://punchng.com/guinea-bissau-junta-to-hold-constitutional-referendum-august-30/

item.title: If men can cheat, women should too, Rita Edochie slams gender bias in marriage
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nollywood actress Rita Edochie criticizes gender bias in marriage, arguing that if men can cheat, women should be afforded the same grace and forgiveness.
Read More: https://punchng.com/if-men-can-cheat-women-should-too-rita-edochie-slams-gender-bias-in-marriage/

item.title: Fury vs AJ could be at Wembley with later start time
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: The blockbuster heavyweight clash between Tyson Fury and Anthony Joshua could still be held at Wembley Stadium, but only if local authorities agree to stage the fight at a later time than usual.

item.title: Court Remands Couple Over Alleged N10m Land Fraud in Rivers
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: A Federal High Court sitting in Port Harcourt, Rivers State, has remanded a couple, Julie Akue Onwuegbu and her husband, Wisdom Onwuegbu, in the Port Harcourt Correctional Centre over an alleged N10 million land fraud. The post Court Remands Couple Over Alleged N10m Land Fraud in Rivers appeared fir

item.title: Huge crowds fill Tehran streets for Khamenei's funeral procession
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Many people were waving Iranian flags and red banners symbolising vengeance for the former supreme leader's war death.

item.title: Tinubu backs FCTA’s TSA exit, commends Wike on Abuja projects
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
President Tinubu defends the FCTA’s exit from the Treasury Single Account, commending Minister Wike for accelerating Abuja’s infrastructure projects and tr
Read More: https://punchng.com/tinubu-backs-fctas-tsa-exit-commends-wike-on-abuja-projects/

item.title: R.T. Briscoe shareholders approve N10 billion capital raise to support expansion
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Shareholders of R.T. Briscoe (Nigeria) Plc have approved a plan to raise up to N10 billion through fresh equity issuance as the company seeks to strengthen its capital base and finance future growth initiatives. The post R.T. Briscoe shareholders approve N10 billion capital raise to support expansio

item.title: PHOTOS: Lagos CAN Leads Peace Walk, Demands Improved Security Nationwide
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The walk began and ended at the Chapel of Christ the Light in Alausa and attracted clergymen, Christian faithful, and concerned citizens who joined the advocacy for peace, unity, and national development. The post PHOTOS: Lagos CAN Leads Peace Walk, Demands Improved Security Nationwide appeared firs

item.title: SEC Mulls New ETF Rules as $16T Boom Disrupts Status Quo
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Bloomberg's Isabelle Lee joins Scarlet Fu, Katie Greifeld, and Eric Balchunas on "Bloomberg ETF IQ." The SEC is signaling a potential rethink of how it oversees ETFs after a recent wave of filings for prediction-market ETFs prompted fresh scrutiny of the existing regulatory framework. (Source: Bloo

item.title: Alia to Tinubu: Benue will “double the numbers” for 2027 as APC unity holds
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Johnbosco Agbakwuru ABUJA — THE Benue State Governor Rev. Fr. Hyacinth Alia on Monday reassured President Bola Tinubu that the All Progressives Congress (APC) remains united in the State despite recent internal disagreements. Governor Alia said he was confident the APC would emerge stronger

item.title: Breaking: Kola Olawuyi’s widow, Abimbola, is dead
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Abimbola Olawuyi, the widow of late investigative broadcaster and journalist Kola Olawuyi, has died, bringing another wave of grief to a family that has lived with the loss of one of Nigeria's most celebrated media figures for nearly two decades.

item.title: Tinubu meets Alia, Suswam behind closed doors as Benue crisis deepens
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: President Bola Tinubu is currently in a private meeting with Benue State Governor Hyacinth Alia and former governor, Senator Gabriel Suswam, at the Presidential Villa in Abuja. The Monday meeting is taking place at a time of rising political tension in the North-Central state. No official details ha

item.title: Super Falcons: Time and venue set for WAFCON 2026 camp
item.dc:creator: Soccernet NG
item.description: The 2026 Women’s Africa Cup of Nations (WAFCON) kicks off later this month, and Nigeria’s Super Falcons are set to compete for an 11th continental crown, Soccernet.ng reports. ​The team’s last outing in Morocco saw them overcome significant challenges to defeat the hosts. This year’s tournament prom

item.title: PFIPC scandal: Atiku’s aide asks Tinubu to show accountability
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: …condemns arrest of Adeyemi’s father Paul Ibe, Media Adviser to former Vice President Atiku Abubakar, has advised President Bola Ahmed Tinubu to show accountability and live up to his responsibilities in handling the scandal-ridden Presidential Foreign Intervention Promotion Council (PFI

item.title: My blessings are with candidates backed by Gov Abdulrazaq: Emir of Ilorin
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: …Says Gov has done very well, urges continuous support for him …I’m certain other aspirants will back our candidature: Speaker Danladi The Emir of Ilorin Dr. Ibrahim Sulu-Gambari CFR on Monday offered his official blessings for the candidates of the All Progressives Congress (APC), hinging his

item.title: KASA Crowned MILO Basketball Champions, Unveils 5 Years Growth Strategy
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: King Amachree Sports Academy (KASA), Obuama, Rivers State, has claimed its first-ever boys’ title at the 26th Nestlé MILO Secondary Schools Basketball Championship, edging defending champions Father O’Connell Science College, Minna, 56-54 in a thrilling final. The national championship, which conclu

item.title: Court sentences two to death in Delta for armed robbery, murder
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Delta State High Court sitting in Effurun, near Warri, has sentenced two men to death after it found them guilty of conspiracy, armed robbery and murder. They are Akpobor Erhianor and Tibra Cornelius, 2nd and 3rd defendants in the case with charge No. EHC/19C/2023, involving the State against them a

item.title: South-East Smallholder Farmers Get 80,640 NADF Fertiliser Bags
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The intervention, implemented by the National Agricultural Development Fund, is aimed at boosting food production. The post South-East Smallholder Farmers Get 80,640 NADF Fertiliser Bags appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: FG urges Ogun, Ondo communities on total support for Dangote Industrial City, Deep Seaport project
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Udeme Akpan The Federal Government has called on host communities in Ogun and Ondo states to give maximum support to the proposed Dangote Industrial City and Deep Seaport project, describing it as a transformative initiative that will create opportunities for economic growth and community develop

item.title: NiMet Trains Professionals On Climate Risk Management
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Nigerian Meteorological Agency (NiMet), in collaboration with the International Livestock Research Institute (ILRI), has commenced a 10-day training of trainers workshop on Climate Risk Management in Agricultural Extension (CRMAE) aimed at equipping agricultural extension professionals wi

item.title: House of Reps receives 2,747 bills, passes 363 in three years
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The lawmakers also defended the growing number of establishment bills, denied allegations of inducement in the legislative process and reaffirmed plans to advance state police and other constitutional reforms. The post House of Reps receives 2,747 bills, passes 363 in three years appeared first on P

item.title: Mexico proud, but 'hurt' after exiting World Cup
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Mexico manager Javier Aguirre said he feels proud but hurt after being eliminated from the FIFA World Cup with a 3-2 loss against England on Sunday night at the Estadio Azteca.

item.title: England survive to beat Mexico in Azteca thriller
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: England survived a raucous Azteca Stadium atmosphere and a red card early in the second half to prevail past co-hosts Mexico 3-2 in a thrilling World Cup round-of-16 clash in Mexico City on Sunday.

item.title: Tearful Neymar retires from Brazil after WC exit
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: An emotional Neymar said his career with Brazil had "finished" after his team's World Cup exit at the hands of Norway in the round of 16 on Sunday.

item.title: Trump: I asked Infantino to 'review' Balogun red
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: President Donald Trump said Monday he called FIFA president Gianni Infantino to review the red card given to USMNT striker Folarin Balogun in the team's match against Bosnia-Herzegovina, calling it a "horrible" call.

item.title: FIFA reject Belgium request; Balogun can play
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: FIFA has ruled a request from the Belgium federation for an explanation of Folarin Balogun's suspended red card as "inadmissible," meaning they have no grounds to appeal the decision and the U.S. forward remains cleared to play against them Monday.

item.title: UEFA: FIFA crossed 'red line' on Balogun ban
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: UEFA have strongly criticized FIFA's decision to suspend USMNT striker Folarin Balogun's red card, thus allowing the United States striker to be available for the World Cup round-of-16 match against Belgium.

item.title: Tottenham sign Tonali for club record $125M fee
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Tottenham have shattered their transfer record for the second time this summer after agreeing a deal worth up to £100m ($133m) to sign Sandro Tonali from Newcastle United.

item.title: 🏆2026-27 UCL season begins mid-World Cup
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: The new Champions League campaign begins 38 days after last season's final. Plenty of unheralded clubs are involved, all with their own fascinating stories.

item.title: Ronaldo: 'This will be my last World Cup'
item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo has admitted that this will be his last World Cup, as he prepares to face Spain with Portugal in Monday's round of 16 clash in Dallas.

item.title: Bandits kill farmer in Sokoto
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: A farmer identified as Mansir has reportedly been killed by suspected bandits while working on his farmland in Isa Local Government Area of Sokoto State. The incident was disclosed in a post shared on X on Monday by security analyst Bakatsine. “This morning, bandits killed a local farmer, iden

item.title: America’s greatest idea is still under threat
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: The United States of America recently turned 250 years old. What a spectacle! The fireworks were amazing, and millions of proud people celebrated across the nation - even around the world. France lit up the Eiffel Tower; Japan had fireworks. French fighter jets flew above New York City with trails o

item.title: Late Alaafin Adeyemi’s son denies controversial PFIPC DG lived in Oyo Palace
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Adeyemi, in a telephone chat with the Nigerian Tribune, stated that the said Adeyemi has no link with Oba Adeyemi’s royal lineage and never... The post Late Alaafin Adeyemi’s son denies controversial PFIPC DG lived in Oyo Palace appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: FG spends N358.3bn on electricity subsidy in Q1 2026 – NERC
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Obas Esiedesa, Abuja ABUJA – The Federal Government incurred an electricity tariff subsidy of N358.32 billion in the first quarter of 2026 as it continued to bridge the gap between cost-reflective electricity tariffs and the rates paid by consumers, according to the latest report by the Nigerian

item.title: FG Urges Media, Influencers To Deny Terrorists Publicity
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The federal government has urged journalists and social media influencers to deny bandits and terrorists the publicity they seek, warning that irresponsible reporting and the spread of misinformation could undermine national unity. The Minister of Information and National Reorientation, Mohammed Idr

item.title: Public Spending in Mexico Ends Prolonged Fall in Fixed Investment
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: A key measure of investments in Mexico snapped back in April driven largely by government spending while private investment lagged, offering scant relief to President Claudia Sheinbaum’s push for stronger economic growth.

item.title: Angry mob lynches, burns young man over alleged motorcycle theft in Kano
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: A young man identified as Ahmad Dankoli was reportedly lynched and his body set ablaze by an angry mob in Balare, Ajingi Local Government Area of Kano State, after he was accused of attempting to steal a motorcycle. The incident occurred at about 9 pm on Sunday, coinciding with the community’s weekl

item.title: The running list: major tech layoffs in 2026 where employers cited AI
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: A running look — in reverse chronological order — at the bigger tech companies that have announced significant layoffs this year with AI as a stated factor.

item.title: VIDEO: Why my ex-husband walked Priscilla down the aisle — Iyabo Ojo
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Nollywood actress Iyabo Ojo has explained why her ex-husband, Ademidun Ojo, walked their daughter, Priscilla Ojo, down the aisle during The post VIDEO: Why my ex-husband walked Priscilla down the aisle — Iyabo Ojo appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: World Cup 2026: Trump Did Not Influenced FIFA’s Decision To Reverse Balogun’s Red Card –Infantino
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: FIFA President Gianni Infantino has confirmed that he received a call from U.S. President Donald Trump regarding the suspension of American striker Folarin Balogun but asserted that the call did not influence the verdict. He stated that he reminded Trump that FIFA’s disciplinary bodies act ind

item.title: Rivers local council bans commercial motorcycle operation
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: While authorities say the ban will improve security and road safety, the decision has sparked fears over lost livelihoods, rising unemployment and transport disruption, with residents urging the council to provide alternative jobs before enforcement begins. The post Rivers local council bans commerc

item.title: Ronaldo Starts Against Spain In Bid To Keep Final World Cup Dream Alive
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo will lead Portugal against Spain in Monday’s FIFA World Cup Round of 16 clash as the veteran forward looks to keep alive his hopes of ending his glittering international career with one final deep tournament run. The 41-year-old captain has been named in Roberto Martínez’s starting

item.title: Trump Bitcoin Reserve Faces Hurdles as Departments Seek Control
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The Trump administration’s plan to create a Strategic Bitcoin Reserve has been complicated by two government departments vying to run it, alongside questions about which has the legal authority to do so.

item.title: Retail Investors Use ETFs as a Tool For Alpha: Zhang
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Sylvia Zhang, executive director and assistant head of global index at E Fund Management, joins Scarlet Fu, Katie Greifeld, and Eric Balchunas on "Bloomberg ETF IQ." They discusses China's investor trends and its booming ETF industry. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Nnorom succeeds Elumelu as Group Chairman of UBA
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Mr Elumelu will retire from the Board of Directors of UBA on 21 August 2026, upon the completion of the 12-year tenure limit prescribed for Non-Executive Directors of Banks by the Central Bank of Nigeria. The post Nnorom succeeds Elumelu as Group Chairman of UBA appeared first on Premium Times Niger

item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Lagos Waste Management Authority (LAWMA) has continued its enforcement and environmental cleanup exercise along the Badagry Expressway, removing illegal structures and vehicles occupying public spaces between Lagos State University (LASU) Gate and Okokomaiko. The agency disclosed this in a post

item.description: Grammy-winning Nigerian singer Wizkid has sparked a fresh supremacy battle with his colleague, Burna Boy, after insinuating that he was chosen to close the Afronation concert in Portugal over Burna Boy, who performed on the opening day, because he is “the best.” DAILY POST reports that B

item.description:

item.title: FRSC Blames Speeding, Fatigue As 32 Die In 3 Road Crashes
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Federal Road Safety Corps (FRSC) has expressed deep sorrow over three fatal road crashes that claimed 32 lives in Kogi, Ogun and Kwara states between Sunday and Monday, renewing its call on motorists to comply with traffic regulations to prevent avoidable deaths. In a statement issued on Monday

item.title: Who should handle your estate plan: Your lawyer or your financial adviser?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The more people who have eyeballs on your situation, the better — but at some point, you are going to need a legal specialist.

item.title: AWS and FlexiSAF Transforming Education in Nigeria with AI Instructor Initiative

item.description: Fourteen years ago, Faiz Bashir began his journey with FlexiSAF with a vision to tackle the administrative challenges Nigerian schools faced with enrolment, grading and record-keeping. Within just twenty days, he developed a software solution that has since been adopted by 120 tertiary institut

item.title: Infantino Denies Interfering In Balogun Decision
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: FIFA president Gianni Infantino has denied interfering in the decision that allowed United States forward Folarin Balogun to remain eligible for the FIFA World Cup, insisting the ruling was made independently by the organisation’s judicial bodies. In a statement issued amid growing criticism of FIFA

item.title: ADC dissolves Kano executives, constitutes caretaker committee
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The African Democratic Congress (ADC) has dissolved its Kano State executive committee and formed a caretaker committee to oversee party affairs.
Read More: https://punchng.com/adc-dissolves-kano-executives-constitutes-caretaker-committee/

item.title: NDC cries out as APC-led Delta Assembly declares Omo-Agege loyalist, Egbetanah’s seat vacant
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Nigerian Democratic Congress, NDC, has criticized the Delta State House of Assembly, led by the All Progressive Congress, APC, for declaring vacant the seat of Honourable Collins Egbetanah, who represents Udu Constituency. Additionally, the House also labeled the lawmaker a persona non grata due

ESG and sustainable funds' saw 95% increase in exposure to nuclear stocks since June 2025. The increase in exposure was likely aided by EU guidance last year that left nuclear arm

item.title: ‘The money would have been life-changing’: My father told my grandmother to slash my inheritance. Is that fair?
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: “I could have bought a home for what I hope will be a growing family.”

item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Kebbi State Police Command says it has foiled an alleged kidnap plot and arrested four suspects accused of threatening to abduct a resident of Jega if he failed to pay a ransom of N30 million. The command’s spokesperson, SP Bashir Usman, disclosed this in a statement issued on Monday. According

item.description: Along several stretches of the expressway, drainage channels meant to carry stormwater are clogged with plastic bottles, nylon bags, food packs and other refuse. item.description: Bookship.org seemed to delay this anticipated partnership again, but tells TechCrunch that it has settled business terms and is working on integration.

item.title: Meet Emmauel Nnorom, new UBA Chairman succeeding Elumelu
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: United Bank for Africa (UBA) Plc has named Emmanuel Nnorom as its new Group Chairman following the retirement of Tony Elumelu. Here are 10 things to know about the incoming UBA chairman: The post Meet Emmauel Nnorom, new UBA Chairman succeeding Elumelu appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Police arrest four over N30m kidnap threat in Kebbi
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Haruna Aliyu BIRNIN KEBBI — The Kebbi State Police Command has arrested four suspects for allegedly threatening to kidnap a resident of Jega town unless he paid a N30 million ransom. The Command’s spokesman, SP Bashir Usman, disclosed this in a statement on Monday. According to him, the sus

item.title: Seven die in Kogi road crash involving Siena bus, stationary truck
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Seven persons have died following a commercial Siena bus crash into a stationary heavy-duty truck along the Okene–Osara–Lokoja Highway in Kogi State. The accident, which occurred on Sunday in Osara, also left three other passengers with varying degrees of injuries. Confirming the incident, the Kogi

item.title: Why I removed FCTA from Treasury Single Account – Tinubu
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has defended his administration’s decision to remove the Federal Capital Territory Administration, FCTA, from the Treasury Single Account, TSA, saying the move was necessary to speed up project delivery and improve infrastructure development in Abuja. Tinubu said the deci

item.title: Portugal vs Spain: Ronaldo, Yamal start as Felix returns in World Cup blockbuster
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Portugal head coach Roberto Martínez has made one change to the side that defeated Croatia, recalling João Félix to the starting XI in place of Rafael Leão, who drops to the bench. The post Portugal vs Spain: Ronaldo, Yamal start as Felix returns in World Cup blockbuster appeared first on Vanguard N

item.title: APM to unveil blueprint to revive economy, tackle insecurity
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Luminous Jannamike ABUJA – The Allied People’s Movement (APM) says it is preparing to unveil a comprehensive governance blueprint aimed at reviving Nigeria’s struggling economy, overhauling the country’s security architecture and restoring public confidence in governance ahead o

item.title: Equities rally adds N3.17trn as Year-to-Date return hits 50%
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Nigerian equities market began the week on a strong footing on Monday as renewed buying interest across banking, industrial and oil... The post Equities rally adds N3.17trn as Year-to-Date return hits 50% appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Ronaldo Starts Against Spain In Bid To Extend Last World Cup
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: While Ronaldo is playing at his last World Cup, the 18-year-old Barcelona winger Yamal is at the opposite end of his career. The post Ronaldo Starts Against Spain In Bid To Extend Last World Cup appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: Elumelu Completes12 Years Maximum Tenure, Retires As UBA Chairman
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: United Bank for Africa (UBA) Plc has announced that its Group chairman Tony Elumelu will retire from the Board effective August 21, 2026, upon completing the 12-year maximum tenure for non-executive directors of banks prescribed by the Central Bank of Nigeria. The Bank stated this in a statement sig

item.title: Ronaldo starts for Portugal against Spain, Silva benched
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo was named in Portugal's starting line-up for the last-16 showdown against Spain on Monday as he bids to extend his last World Cup. The post Ronaldo starts for Portugal against Spain, Silva benched appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Tinubu orders probe of Meta, Google, X, AI platforms over alleged malpractices
item.dc:creator: ICIR Nigeria
item.description: PRESIDENT Bola Tinubu has directed the Federal Competition and Consumer Protection Commission (FCCPC) to investigate major global technology companies and Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) platforms over allegations of anti-competitive practices and the unlawful use of content produced by Nige

item.title: Flavio Bolsonaro asks Trump to delay tariffs on Brazil until after election
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: President Lula accuses Jair Bolsonaro's son, now a presidential hopeful, of helping trigger proposed US tariffs.

item.title: Ticket prices continue to fall for USA-Belgium World Cup last 16 clash
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Get-in prices for the round of 16 game at Seattle Stadium fall by 27 percent over the past three days.

item.title: Edo govt warns cultists against 7/7 celebration
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The Edo State Government on Monday warned suspected cultists, particularly members of the Black Axe confraternity... The post Edo govt warns cultists against 7/7 celebration appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Florence Ajimobi Begins Diplomatic Mission In Austria
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Florence Ajimobi Begins Diplomatic Mission in Austria Nigeria’s Ambassador and Permanent Representative to Austria, Florence Ajimobi, has formally commenced her diplomatic mission in Vienna after presenting her Copie d’Usage to Austria’s Chief of Protocol, Ambassador Maximilian Hen

item.title: After 15 years, Bukky Wright premieres ‘Omotara Johnson’ sequel in Lagos
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The veteran actress premiered the long-awaited sequel to her 2008 Yoruba crime classic ahead of its nationwide cinema release on 10 July. The post After 15 years, Bukky Wright premieres ‘Omotara Johnson’ sequel in Lagos appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Infantino Defends FIFA Independence After Balogun Red Card Suspension Backlash
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: FIFA President Gianni Infantino has defended the independence of the governing body’s judicial process following widespread criticism over the suspension of United States striker Folarin Balogun. Infantino, in a statement released on Monday, said the FIFA Disciplinary Committee operates indepe

item.title: Joyce misses out on heavyweight title shot as Gassiev opponent confirmed
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Murat Gassiev's first defence of his WBA heavyweight title will go ahead on Saturday after a replacement opponent was found for the Russian.
